commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8a0a5c733ea5d9581898ec7ed55c257c0db6c065)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared Responses replay ID normalizer in `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`, applies it to overlong reasoning and assistant message IDs, and adds a request-builder regression test.

Reproducibility: unclear. for the full user path. Source inspection shows the raw request-builder seam can preserve overlong reasoning IDs, but current main’s registered Copilot wrapper sanitizes payloads before send and the PR proof does not exercise that wrapper or a live Telegram/Copilot route.
commands.review_followup: Maintainer review is needed to decide whether the observed failure is a real wrapper-bypass gap and to reject or rework the hashed reasoning replay approach before merge.

target.title: fix(agents): hash oversized Responses reasoning replay IDs
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: GitHub Copilot native Responses can reject resumed Telegram direct sessions when persisted reasoning replay item IDs exceed the provider's 64-character `input[].id` limit.
- Solution: Normalize oversized Responses replay item IDs through one helper before dispatch.
- What changed: Replayed reasoning IDs now hash to an `rs_` ID when overlong, and assistant message replay IDs reuse the same helper.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Native Codex Responses still omits replay item IDs, and valid short provider IDs are preserved unchanged.

## Motivation

- The affected Telegram direct session accepted inbound messages but failed before assistant output with `Invalid 'input[112].id': string too long. Expected a string with maximum length 64, but got a string with length 383 instead.`

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #85197
- Related #74665
- Related #81520
- Related #81534
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: GitHub Copilot native Responses request building no longer replays a persisted 383-character reasoning item ID into `input[].id`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw worktree on Linux with Node v24.15.0, using the real `buildOpenAIResponsesParams` request-builder seam for `github-copilot/gpt-5.5`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` imported `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`, built a GitHub Copilot Responses payload from an assistant `thinkingSignature` whose reasoning item `id` was 383 characters, and printed the outbound reasoning item ID length.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Console output from the direct request-builder seam:

```json
{
  "originalIdLength": 383,
  "replayedId": "rs_ba2597601eb52288",
  "replayedIdLength": 19,
  "withinLimit": true
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The outbound replayed reasoning item ID is hashed to `rs_ba2597601eb52288`, length 19, which is below the provider's observed 64-character limit.
- What was not tested: No live Telegram or GitHub Copilot provider rerun was performed because the failing evidence depends on a private persisted Telegram session and credentials.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Redacted runtime log excerpt from the affected setup:

```shell
embedded run prompt start: provider=github-copilot api=openai-responses endpoint=github-copilot-native route=native
context-overflow-precheck: route=fits estimatedPromptTokens=56663 overflowTokens=0
embedded_run_agent_end: isError=true error="OpenAI API error (400): 400 Invalid 'input[112].id': string too long. Expected a string with maximum length 64, but got a string with length 383 instead." model=gpt-5.5 provider=github-copilot rawErrorHash=sha256:2279064209cc
message dispatch completed: channel=telegram outcome=completed
```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `convertResponsesMessages()` parsed persisted JSON `thinkingSignature` blocks as replayable Responses reasoning items and preserved their `id` whenever Responses item ID replay was enabled. GitHub Copilot native Responses uses that replay path, so overlong historical reasoning IDs reached `input[].id` unchanged.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing Copilot replay tests covered overlong tool/function replay IDs and assistant message IDs, but not persisted reasoning item IDs.
- Contributing context (if known): Prior replay fixes normalized tool/function item IDs; the reasoning replay provenance fix did not add the same max-length normalization for `reasoning.id`.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: A GitHub Copilot `openai-responses` payload built from a persisted reasoning `thinkingSignature` with a 383-character `id` emits an `rs_` hashed replay ID no longer than 64 characters.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The failure happens during request construction before provider generation, so the request-builder seam catches the bug without requiring live Telegram credentials or a private session file.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None for reasoning item IDs.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Telegram direct sessions with overlong persisted Copilot Responses reasoning replay IDs can continue instead of failing before assistant output.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[persisted reasoning id length 383] -> [Responses input[].id unchanged] -> [provider 400]

After:
[persisted reasoning id length 383] -> [rs_<hash> replay id] -> [request stays within item-id limit]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local worktree
- Runtime/container: Node v24.15.0
- Model/provider: github-copilot/gpt-5.5
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram direct session evidence; local request-builder verification
- Relevant config (redacted): GitHub Copilot native Responses route with a redacted private Telegram session

### Steps

1. Build a GitHub Copilot Responses payload from an assistant message with a persisted JSON `thinkingSignature` reasoning item.
2. Use an overlong 383-character reasoning item `id`.
3. Inspect the emitted `reasoning` item in `params.input`.

### Expected

- The replayed reasoning item ID is at most 64 characters.

### Actual

- Before this patch, the 383-character ID was replayed unchanged and the provider rejected `input[112].id`.
- After this patch, the ID is hashed to an `rs_` replay ID with length 19.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Applied the BUG-034 patch, formatted the touched files, ran the request-builder console proof, ran the focused agent transport Vitest file, ran `git diff --check`, and ran autoreview.
- Edge cases checked: Valid short replay IDs remain unchanged by the helper; overlong reasoning and assistant message replay IDs hash with the intended prefix.
- What you did **not** verify: Live Telegram replay against the private affected session and live GitHub Copilot credentials.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Hashing an oversized replay ID could change provider-side item association for that historical reasoning item.
  - Mitigation: The provider already rejects the oversized ID, so hashing only applies to the currently broken path while preserving valid short IDs unchanged.
target.author: galiniliev

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
